raw_consumer_statement:
I booked a prepaid car rental through XXXX, which was fulfilled by XXXX for {$500.00}. When I arrived at the XXXX location in Florida on XXXX XXXX XXXX, I was told I could not pick up the vehicle due to being under XXXX XXXX XXXX despite having already paid for the rental and never being asked for my age during booking. 

I offered to transfer the rental to my travel companion ( who was over XXXX ) or downgrade to a lower-tier vehicle I was eligible for. Both requests were denied by XXXX staff. Instead, a XXXX employee told me my best option was to contact XXXX for a refund and rent a different car elsewhere. I followed that advice in good faith. 

XXXX submitted a refund request, but XXXX denied it. I was then forced to pay over {$600.00} out-of-pocket for a new rental. I disputed the charge with American Express multiple times, explaining that I was denied service and misled by the merchant. AmEx sided with the merchant based solely on their terms and conditions, ignoring the fact that I never received the product and followed the employee 's advice. 

I am asking that AmEx issue a refund for the {$500.00} charge. I never received the service, and their dispute resolution process failed to account for the full context and financial impact.
